generated_oml:
instance GantryCrane : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Laboratory‑scale gantry crane with horizontal gantry frame, vertical rails, trolley rail (X axis), vertical actuator / hoist (Y axis), and a load‑bearing carriage."
]
instance HorizontalGantryFrame : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Horizontal gantry frame of the laboratory crane."
]
instance VerticalRails : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Vertical rails supporting the crane structure."
]
instance TrolleyRailX : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Trolley rail along the X axis."
]
instance VerticalActuatorHoistY : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Vertical actuator / hoist along the Y axis."
]
instance LoadBearingCarriage : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Load‑bearing carriage that carries the load."
]

instance CartMotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "TMC4671 Eval controller board driving the cart motor over a CANopen network."
]
instance HoistMotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "TMC4671 Eval controller board driving the hoist motor over CANopen."
]
instance LoadReleaseElectromagnetActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromagnet mounted at the hoist end to release the load, controlled by the hoist controller."
]

instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Encoder measuring hoist swing motion."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SwingAngleData
]
instance WindSpeedAnemometer : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Anemometer recording wind speed affecting the crane."
    DTDFVocab:producedData WindSpeedData
]
instance CargoPuckVisionCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vision system camera tracking the position of the cargo puck."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VisionPuckData
]

instance SwingAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]
instance WindSpeedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom WindSpeedAnemometer
]
instance VisionPuckData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CargoPuckVisionCamera
]
instance SimulationResultsTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
]
instance SimulationEngineComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Simulation engine component that produces simulation results."
]

instance CADModelGantry : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD geometry of the gantry crane derived from Autodesk Fusion360."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CADModelGenerationEnabler
]
instance KinematicModelGantry : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical kinematic model mapping joint inputs to Cartesian positions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryGeneratorEnabler
]
instance SimulationResults : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulation results of crane motion persisted in TimescaleDB."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler, DataLoggingEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulationResultsTransmitted
]
instance SwingAngleMeasurements : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑tagged swing angle readings stored in TimescaleDB."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAcquisitionModuleEnabler, DataLoggingEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData SwingAngleData
]
instance WindSpeedMeasurements : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑tagged wind speed readings stored in TimescaleDB."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAcquisitionModuleEnabler, DataLoggingEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData WindSpeedData
]
instance VisionPuckMeasurements : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑tagged cargo puck position data persisted in TimescaleDB."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAcquisitionModuleEnabler, DataLoggingEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData VisionPuckData
]

instance DataAcquisitionModuleEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component that collects sensor data via MQTT from the Arduino microcontroller."
    DTDFVocab:enables ShaftPositionMonitoringService, LoadDisplacementMonitoringService
]
instance TrajectoryGeneratorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Analytical kinematic model used by the trajectory planning service."
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryPlanningService
]
instance ValidationMessageBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ActiveMQ broker providing simulated and sensor messages for continuous validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ValidationService
]
instance DataLoggingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "TimescaleDB instance that archives all sensor and simulation data for historical analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataLoggingService
]
instance GrafanaDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Grafana dashboard interface that visualizes operational metrics from InfluxDB and TimescaleDB."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationDashboardService
]
instance InfluxDBEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "InfluxDB provisioned to ingest sensor telemetry for visual analytics."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationDashboardService
]
instance CADModelGenerationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Toolchain that converts the CAD model into an analytical kinematic model."
]
instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python simulation engine that numerically integrates crane dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineComponent
    DTDFVocab:hasInput SimulationResults
]

instance ShaftPositionMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time monitoring of gantry shaft positions and load displacements."
    DTDFVocab:provides ShaftPositionInsight
]
instance LoadDisplacementMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time monitoring of load displacement."
    DTDFVocab:provides LoadDisplacementInsight
]
instance TrajectoryPlanningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimal trajectory planning for cart and hoist motors derived from the kinematic model."
    DTDFVocab:provides TrajectoryOptimizationInsight
]
instance ValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous validation comparing live sensor data to simulated values."
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationAlarms
]
instance DataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Historical data logging of all sensor and simulation data to TimescaleDB."
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalDataInsight
]
instance VisualizationDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboards using Grafana to display operational metrics."
    DTDFVocab:provides DashboardVisualizationInsight
]

instance ShaftPositionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into current shaft positions derived from sensor data."
]
instance LoadDisplacementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into load displacement measurements."
]
instance TrajectoryOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized trajectory plan for crane motion."
]
instance ValidationAlarms : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Alarms triggered when validation distance metric exceeds predefined thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance HistoricalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into historical trends of operational parameters."
]
instance DashboardVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of operational metrics on Grafana dashboards."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Control Application sends control commands to the Physical Crane through the Physical Crane Interface, a Python program that connects directly to the crane controllers. The command flow uses the crane’s native controller interface; no explicit authentication, validation, or conflict‑resolution mechanisms are described."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of the exemplar Digital Twin follows a three‑phase sequence. In the **Design and Modeling** phase, the twin is conceived using AutomationML for the indoor air quality exemplar, and by extension a domain‑specific modeling language for the greenhouse uses a similar generic model. This phase establishes the conceptual twin, refines sensor mappings for the Raspberry‑Pi or greenhouse sensors, and defines the simulation and prediction capabilities. In the **Implementation and Deployment** phase, the twin and the physical system are containerized or virtualized; Docker‑compose files (present in the GitHub repository) orchestrate a set of services, which include an InfluxDB time‑series database for sensor data, an ActiveMQ message broker to shuttle telemetry, and a virtual machine image that pre‑installs all software components. Finally, in the **Operation and Validation** phase, the twin runs continuously, providing real‑time monitoring via a Grafana dashboard and performing continuous validation—distance metrics between real and simulated values are calculated automatically; if the metric falls outside an acceptable threshold, the twin triggers an alert or an adaptive change to the physical system. The transition between these stages is governed by the release cycle in the repository, with clear packaging of each phase’s artefacts for reproducibility."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is maintained by a Continuous Validation Service that continuously compares executed trajectories with simulated trajectories. It calculates a distance metric between the real and simulated points, compares the value with a predefined acceptable threshold, and, if the threshold is breached, automatically notifies operators via the dashboard while logging the discrepancy for potential model refinement. Operators can then review the data and adjust the model parameters to improve accuracy."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Within the Digital Twin, an MQTT broker mediates signaling between services such as the Trajectory Generator Service, Simulation Service, and Continuous Validation Service. The document does not provide details on external network topology, bandwidth, latency, redundancy or security protocols for communication between the digital twin and the physical crane."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "All components are deployed on a single on‑premises computer located in the laboratory. The core services (TimescaleDB, MQTT Broker, and Grafana Dashboard) are containerized using Docker, enabling isolated and reproducible environments. The Control Application, Physical Crane Interface, Logging Application, Trajectory Generator Service, Simulation Service, and Continuous Validation Service run as native Python processes or dockerized containers on the same machine. This edge deployment eliminates cloud dependencies, ensures low‑latency communication, and satisfies the requirement for an on‑premises, single‑site deployment model. No virtualization beyond Docker containers is described, and the system is not designed for multi‑site or cloud‑based scaling in the provided documentation."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "All data exchange occurs internally via MQTT (or ActiveMQ) with no described integration to external enterprise systems or other digital twins. The twin does not use standard interoperability protocols such as OPC‑UA or AutomationML for external communication."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The exemplar adopts the C4 Model to document system architecture—context, container, component, and dynamic diagrams are prepared to illustrate the relationships between the green‑house hardware, sensors, Twin services, the InfluxDB backend, and the ActiveMQ broker. The digital twin itself is structured using AutomationML, a recognized industry specification for modeling mechanical and electromechanical systems, which facilitates interoperability with other Engineering design tools. The data layer utilizes InfluxDB, which follows the open‑source line protocol and Grafana capabilities, ensuring compatibility with a broad ecosystem of monitoring solutions. Communication between the physical system (Raspberry‑Pi or greenhouse controllers) and the twin services uses a standard message broker, ActiveMQ, which supports MQTT‑style topics, thereby aligning with common IoT messaging protocols. No proprietary or non‑standard compliance frameworks are explicitly referenced in the documentation."
]
